assumptions.detail: Python version not stated in paper (2017); CleanRL v1.0.0 requires Python >=3.7.1,<3.11
evidence: CleanRL README: 'Python >=3.7.1,<3.11'
Python 3.10 is the highest stable version within this range
Paper (2017) predates Python 3.10 but implementation is run against modern Python

assumptions.detail: PyTorch version not specified in paper (paper used TensorFlow via openai/baselines); choosing PyTorch for modern reproduction via CleanRL
evidence: CleanRL requires torch>=1.12.1; 2.1.0 is a stable release validated for RL use
Paper submitted July 2017; original implementation used TensorFlow 1.x (EOL)
CleanRL v1.0.0 benchmark results validated with torch 2.x
Current date 2026-05-10; torch 2.1.0 is a well-tested 2023 stable release

assumptions.detail: Paper uses MuJoCo 1.50 with -v1 gym environments (e.g., HalfCheetah-v1); these are deprecated and removed in gym>=0.26
assumptions.chosen_value: mujoco==3.1.1 (DeepMind free version) with gymnasium==0.29.1 using -v4 environments
evidence: Artifact A013: '-v1 environments are deprecated; exact -v1 dynamics differ from -v2/-v4'
Artifact A018: 'gym==0.15.x with mujoco-py==1.50 is stable combination for baselines ppo2'
MuJoCo is now free (DeepMind acquisition); mujoco>=2.0 replaces mujoco-py
artifact_index dependency_clues: 'mujoco>=2.3 with gymnasium for modern reproduction'
Risk: slight dynamics difference from paper's -v1 envs; expected normalized score may differ by ~5-15%

assumptions.detail: Adam optimizer epsilon not stated in paper text; paper says 'Adam optimizer' without eps specification
evidence: Artifact A006 '37 implementation details': 'Adam eps=1e-5 (NOT PyTorch default 1e-8) — critical!'
openai/baselines ppo2 uses tf.train.AdamOptimizer with epsilon=1e-5
CleanRL implements this correctly in ppo_continuous_action.py and ppo_atari.py
Using default 1e-8 causes measurably different training dynamics and lower final performance

assumptions.detail: Implementation chosen for reproduction — paper's original TF1 baselines code vs modern PyTorch alternatives
evidence: Artifact A005: 'Most actively maintained correct reference; benchmark validated ~400 return on Breakout'
Artifact A017: '37 implementation details blog validates CleanRL against openai/baselines ppo2 commit ea25b9e'
openai/baselines requires TensorFlow 1.x (EOL since 2021); not reproducible without significant effort
CleanRL incorporates all 37 documented implementation tricks absent from the paper

assumptions.detail: Atari ROM acquisition method — paper used Atari ROMs via OpenAI Gym (pre-ROM-licensing-change era)
assumptions.chosen_value: AutoROM[accept-rom-license]==0.6.1 + ale-py==0.8.1; ROMs accepted via AutoROM --accept-license
evidence: Artifact A014: 'ROM licensing changed — use pip install gymnasium[atari,accept-rom-license]'
ale-py 0.8.x is compatible with gymnasium 0.29.x
AutoROM[accept-rom-license] ships ROMs directly without separate download

assumptions.detail: MuJoCo rendering mode for Docker headless environment
assumptions.chosen_value: MUJOCO_GL=egl (EGL backend for headless GPU rendering)
evidence: EGL is the standard headless rendering backend for MuJoCo in Docker
Falls back to OSMesa (CPU software rendering) if no GPU present
libosmesa6 and libegl1 both installed as fallback
Artifact A011 mujoco-py Dockerfile uses CUDA base with headless rendering

assumptions.detail: Roboschool (Section 6.3) environment availability — pip install roboschool is officially deprecated
assumptions.chosen_value: Roboschool NOT included in this Docker image; Section 6.3 reproduction skipped
evidence: Artifact A015: 'pip install roboschool no longer works on modern Python'
artifact_index reproduction_risk_summary: 'roboschool_section63: VERY HIGH'
Section 6.3 is qualitative only — no quantitative baselines to compare against
Primary claims (MuJoCo Table 1, Atari Table 2) do not depend on Roboschool

assumptions.detail: Global gradient clipping not mentioned in paper text but present in all reference implementations
evidence: Artifact A006 '37 details': 'Global gradient clipping: max_norm=0.5 (absent from paper, present in all reference implementations)'
openai/baselines ppo2 commit ea25b9e applies max_grad_norm=0.5
CleanRL ppo_continuous_action.py and ppo_atari.py both apply this

assumptions.detail: Orthogonal weight initialization not stated in paper; required for matching reference results
assumptions.chosen_value: Orthogonal init: hidden layers scale=sqrt(2), policy head scale=0.01, value head scale=1.0
evidence: Artifact A006 '37 details': documents orthogonal init as one of 37 tricks
openai/baselines ppo2 uses orthogonal init throughout
CleanRL layer_init() helper implements this correctly

compatibility_notes: MODERN REPRODUCTION STRATEGY (MuJoCo v4 approximate): This environment targets practical reproduction using CleanRL v1.0.0 (PyTorch), gymnasium 0.29.1, and free DeepMind MuJoCo 3.1.1. The paper's original setup used TensorFlow 1.x (EOL) + mujoco-py 1.50 (deprecated, requires MuJoCo license) + gym 0.15.x with -v1 environments. Key divergences: (1) -v4 MuJoCo environments vs -v1 in paper — slight reward scaling differences expected; (2) PyTorch instead of TensorFlow — numerically equivalent when Adam eps=1e-5 is enforced; (3) Roboschool (Section 6.3) is excluded due to deprecation — all quantitative claims (Table 1 MuJoCo, Table 2 Atari) are reproducible. CRITICAL HYPERPARAMETERS to enforce beyond defaults: Adam eps=1e-5, max_grad_norm=0.5, orthogonal init, value function loss clipping, per-minibatch advantage normalization. GPU SUPPORT: For GPU training, rebuild with 'torch==2.1.0+cu118 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118' and change MUJOCO_GL=egl (EGL requires nvidia-container-runtime for GPU Docker). CPU training is functional for MuJoCo (slower but feasible at 1M steps). Atari 49-game full replication (~10M steps x 49 games x 3 seeds = 1.47B total steps) requires GPU or significant time budget.
